name: Harris Matrix Moral Philosophy Topology
mechanism: Harris Matrix (Archaeological Stratigraphy) in archaeology operates through the interaction of stratigraphic unit (context) as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through depositional, interfacial, and structural contexts, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—physical superposition and cutting relations order contexts—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by internally consistent site chronology. This process is bounded by single excavation, intact stratification, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.
application: When transplanted to moral philosophy, the mechanism from archaeology suggests that the core units of moral philosophy undergo an analogous evolutionary process. The variation mechanism (depositional, interfacial, and structural contexts) maps onto the generative processes in moral philosophy, while the selection pressure (physical superposition and cutting relations order contexts) corresponds to the evaluative forces that determine which innovations, strategies, or structures persist in the target domain. This cross-domain mapping predicts that moral philosophy systems will exhibit phase transitions, path dependencies, and fitness landscape topologies that mirror those observed in archaeology, but expressed through domain-specific substrates and timescales. The boundary conditions (single excavation, intact stratification) constrain where the analogy holds and where it breaks down.
prediction: Systems in moral philosophy designed using archaeology structural principles will show 25% reduction in failure rates compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=100 cases over a 8-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

name: Euler's Theorem Forensic Science Ecology
mechanism: Euler's Theorem (Graph Theory) in graph theory operates through the interaction of graph edge traversal as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through vertex degree parity, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—Eulerian circuit exists iff all vertices have even degree—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by existence of a single traversal covering all edges. This process is bounded by connected graph, undirected or directed variant, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. prediction: Systems in forensic science designed using graph theory structural principles will show 20% higher performance scores compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=120 cases over a 6-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

name: Minimax Regret Quantum Computing System
mechanism: Minimax Regret in decision theory operates through the interaction of decision under uncertainty as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through regret matrix across actions and states of the world, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—choose action minimizing worst-case regret—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by maximum regret minimized. This process is bounded by finite action and state space, no probabilities assumed, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.
application: When transplanted to quantum computing, the mechanism from decision theory suggests that the core units of quantum computing undergo an analogous evolutionary process. This cross-domain mapping predicts that quantum computing systems will exhibit phase transitions, path dependencies, and fitness landscape topologies that mirror those observed in decision theory, but expressed through domain-specific substrates and timescales. The boundary conditions (finite action and state space, no probabilities assumed) constrain where the analogy holds and where it breaks down.
prediction: Systems in quantum computing designed using decision theory structural principles will show 28% increase in output quality compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=200 cases over a 12-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

name: HPA Axis Hormonal Feedback Loop Structural Engineering Model
mechanism: HPA Axis Hormonal Feedback Loop in endocrinology operates through the interaction of hormone concentration (CRH, ACTH, cortisol) as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through stressor input and glucocorticoid receptor sensitivity, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—cortisol negative feedback suppresses CRH and ACTH release—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by return to basal cortisol setpoint. This process is bounded by receptor downregulation under chronic stress, circadian rhythm, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.

This cross-domain mapping predicts that structural engineering systems will exhibit phase transitions, path dependencies, and fitness landscape topologies that mirror those observed in endocrinology, but expressed through domain-specific substrates and timescales. The boundary conditions (receptor downregulation under chronic stress, circadian rhythm) constrain where the analogy holds and where it breaks down.
prediction: Systems in structural engineering designed using endocrinology structural principles will show 25% reduction in failure rates compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=200 cases over a 12-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

name: Capillary Bridge Failure Cascade Ecology Topology
mechanism: Capillary Bridge Failure Cascade in sandcastle engineering operates through the interaction of liquid bridge between two adjacent sand grains as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through water content, grain size distribution, evaporation rate, wind exposure, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—bridge networks that redistribute stress after individual bridge failure avoid catastrophic collapse—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by time to structural failure under drying conditions. This process is bounded by pendular-to-funicular regime transition, grain surface hydrophobicity, pore air pressure, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. name: Isothermal Metamorphism Bond Sintering Chronobiology Calculus
mechanism: Isothermal Metamorphism Bond Sintering in avalanche science operates through the interaction of ice neck forming between adjacent snow grains via vapor diffusion as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through temperature, grain size distribution, and contact geometry control sintering rate and resulting bond strength between grains, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—well-sintered layers with high bond-to-grain ratio gain shear strength; poorly bonded layers remain as persistent weak layers—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by shear strength gain rate sufficient to support overlying slab loads before next loading event. This process is bounded by temperature gradient re-establishment destroying sinter bonds, rain-freeze crusts creating impermeable barriers, extremely cold temperatures slowing sintering kinetics, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.
application: When transplanted to chronobiology, the mechanism from avalanche science suggests that the core units of chronobiology undergo an analogous evolutionary process. The variation mechanism (temperature, grain size distribution, and contact geometry control sintering rate and resulting bond strength between grains) maps onto the generative processes in chronobiology, while the selection pressure (well-sintered layers with high bond-to-grain ratio gain shear strength; poorly bonded layers remain as persistent weak layers) corresponds to the evaluative forces that determine which innovations, strategies, or structures persist in the target domain. This cross-domain mapping predicts that chronobiology systems will exhibit phase transitions, path dependencies, and fitness landscape topologies that mirror those observed in avalanche science, but expressed through domain-specific substrates and timescales. The boundary conditions (temperature gradient re-establishment destroying sinter bonds, rain-freeze crusts creating impermeable barriers, extremely cold temperatures slowing sintering kinetics) constrain where the analogy holds and where it breaks down.
prediction: Systems in chronobiology designed using avalanche science structural principles will show 35% improvement in resilience measures compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=200 cases over a 6-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.
